channel.title: Tech Insights
channel.description: Daily curated news on AI, developer tools, and the tech industry

item.title: I built an offline-first Markdown editor that runs as a single HTML file
item.description: Most Markdown editors today assume cloud sync, Electron, or heavy installations. I wanted something simpler. I wanted a Markdown editor that: Works fully offline Opens local .md files directly...

item.title: 7 Coder Words: Lessons from Building a PWA Word Puzzle for Coders and Nerds
item.description: 7 CODER W🙂RDS is a word puzzle game for people interested in computer science in general. You are given 7 clues and you need to guess the word behind each clue by combining shuffled word tiles....

item.title: "Why I stopped trusting npm audit (and built my own)"
item.description: Generate a CycloneDX SBOM and deterministic, audit-ready risk report from your package-lock.json. You run npm audit. It says “47 vulnerabilities.” Cool. Which ones actually matter? The one in your...

item.title: Workspace 2026 : May the Fourth Be With You — The Rise of Ephemeral Dev Environments
item.description: Introduction Let's have some fun and ask ChatGPT the following question : From the perspective of software system architecture in 2026, what would the Jedi and Sith approaches respectively be...

item.title: AI Captions Are Useful, But Field Reports Still Need Human Review
item.description: AI captions can make field reporting faster. They can also create risk if teams treat them as final truth. That is the tension. In field documentation, a caption is not just a convenience. It can...

item.title: Seeking Testers: GNSS App
item.description: I created "You Are Here," a GNSS app that uses your Android device's navigation hardware (_not _wifi or cellular) + your magnetometer to tell you about the satellites around us. Visit the link at...

item.title: I built a 4-week hackathon focused entirely on real-world debugging instead of Leetcode problems
item.description: Most coding platforms train engineers to solve isolated algorithm problems. But in real engineering, you rarely reverse linked lists. You debug production systems. You trace issues across files....

item.title: Where misunderstood with Monoliths and Kubernetes: Benchmark
item.description: 85% of teams migrating to Kubernetes from monoliths report no statistically significant latency improvement in the first 12 months, yet 72% still push the migration anyway. Our 10-iteration...

item.title: How I was introduced to SQL window function
item.description: I was called at my firm for having to create a analytics dashboard for a multiTenet CRM dashboard where the magnitude of rows were in thousands and the efficiency i could not blame postgress or...

item.title: Seeking Testers: Chime Ball
item.description: I have created an Android app called "Chime Ball." The idea for this app = SUPER SIMPLICITY, for kids. Balls are dropped into a play area which can be arranged on a stealthy treble clef-ish shelf...

item.title: The latest AI news we announced in April 2026
item.description: mp4 featuring an underwater video and a mobile AI video mockup.

item.title: Elon Musk’s only AI expert witness at the OpenAI trial fears an AGI arms race
item.description: Stuart Russell is a long-time AI researcher who thinks governments need to restrain frontier labs.

item.title: The creator of Roomba is back with a furry robot companion
item.description: Colin Angle, the maker of the Roomba and the man who helped put 50 million household robots into people's homes, is back with a new robot. But this one is designed as a companion, not a cleaner....

item.title: The Pixel 11 could be the next victim of the RAM shortage
item.description: Google's next round of Pixel phones could be a downgrade compared to the Pixel 10 lineup due to the ongoing RAM shortage. Leaked specs for the Pixel 11 lineup shared by MysticLeaks include a...

item.title: Sierra raises $950M as the race to own enterprise AI gets serious
item.description: The raise gives Sierra more than $1 billion to work with — capital the company says it will use to become the "global standard" for AI-powered customer experiences.

item.title: Elon Musk sent ominous texts to Greg Brockman, Sam Altman after asking for a settlement, OpenAI claims
item.description: Musk texted OpenAI's president and co-founder saying that he and CEO Sam Altman "will be the most hated men in America."

item.title: Progress Patches Critical MOVEit Automation Bug Enabling Authentication Bypass
item.description: Progress Software has released updates to address two security flaws in MOVEit Automation, including a critical bug that could result in an authentication bypass. MOVEit Automation (formerly...

item.title: The best Star Wars Day deals
item.description: May the Fourth be with us all on this Star Wars Day. While things have been relatively quiet with the multimedia gold mine that is Star Wars, with just some murmurs about upcoming movies (The...

item.title: Anthropic and OpenAI are both launching joint ventures for enterprise AI services
item.description: Both Anthropic and OpenAI have partnered with asset managers to more aggressively market their enterprise AI products.

item.title: Week one of the Musk v. Altman trial: What it was like in the room
item.description: This story originally appeared in The Algorithm, our weekly newsletter on AI. To get stories like this in your inbox first, sign up here. Two of the most powerful people in AI—Sam Altman and Elon...

item.title: Live updates from Elon Musk and Sam Altman’s court battle over the future of OpenAI
item.description: Sam Altman and Elon Musk are facing off in a high-stakes trial that could alter the future of OpenAI and its most well-known product, ChatGPT. In 2024, Musk filed a lawsuit accusing OpenAI of...

item.title: Amazon’s trying to turn its massive shipping operation into another AWS
item.description: Amazon might have a part in delivering all kinds of stuff to your door soon, as its massive shipping network opens up to other companies outside its marketplace, more directly competing with...

item.title: Dara Khosrowshahi on replacing Uber drivers — and himself — with AI
item.description: Today, I’m talking with Uber CEO Dara Khosrowshahi. It’s become something of an annual tradition to have Dara join us in the studio when he comes to New York for Uber’s big GO-GET event every...

item.title: Tesla hits Musk’s threshold for ‘safe unsupervised’ driving
item.description: We've crossed yet another one of Elon Musk's self-driving thresholds. Tesla's fleet of vehicles using the company's Full Self-Driving (Supervised) system has driven over 10 billion miles,...

item.title: ⚡ Weekly Recap: AI-Powered Phishing, Android Spying Tool, Linux Exploit, GitHub RCE & More
item.description: This week, the shadows moved faster than the patches. While most teams were still triaging last month’s alerts, attackers had already turned control panels into kill switches, kernels into open...

item.title: 5 days only: Bring a partner or colleague and get 50% off a second TechCrunch Disrupt 2026 pass
item.description: The BOGO offer is live. For a limited time, buy one pass to TechCrunch Disrupt 2026 and get 50% off a second of the same ticket type. Offer ends this Friday, May 8. Save here.

item.title: GameStop makes $56 billion offer to acquire eBay
item.description: GameStop has made an offer to acquire eBay for $56 billion, which comes as part of GameStop CEO Ryan Cohen's plan to transform the online marketplace into a "legit competitor to Amazon," he tells...

item.title: DoorDash adds AI tools to speed up merchant onboarding, edit photos of dishes
item.description: DoorDash on Monday added new AI-powered tools that let merchants speed up onboarding, edit photos to make dishes look better, and create new websites from existing content.

item.title: Tailoring AI solutions for health care needs
item.description: The AI market is full of big promises of grand transformation. Health care is a prime target for those promises, beset as it is by financial pressures, labor shortages, and the growing burden of...

item.title: Hisense aggressively cuts the price of its RGB LED TV on release day
item.description: The Hisense UR9 - the first RGB LED TV to be released this year - is now available for much less than originally revealed. The 65-inch UR9 is now $1,999, while the 75-inch model is $2,999, and the...
